135 - Diffuse Sales Objections With This Technique - YouTube.mp4
Now in this video, you're going to learn how to defuse any objections that your potential customers bring up in your sales conversations. Now I have to ask you this: when a potential customer brings up an objection, like your price is too high, or we don't have the budget for this, or is this a scam, or let me think it over, or leave a brochure. What starts going through your mind? The old selling mindset comes back. Of course, you go into objection handling mode. That's what most of us have been taught. Right, we get impatient. We sometimes cut them off before they finish. Your sentence. We've heard this objection before, and doggone it, we know how to cut them off and overcome it with facts and logic to prove and support our solution.
We say things like, 'Well, Mrs. Jones. I mean, I know how you feel, and others have felt the same way, and and this is what they found The Phil Felt-Found theory that's been around since 1950. What is the potential problem, though, when you use these traditional selling techniques from the old sales gurus? Have you considered that most of your potential customers have heard your traditional selling techniques before, with many other salespeople. Ask yourself, how do you feel when salespeople who are trying to sell you something use them on you? Can I suggest something to you? Concerns are not objections. I would suggest not handling them as if they were, otherwise you're likely to lose the relationship that you've already built. Now is not the time to try to sell them or persuade them why you're right.
It's time to listen and validate what they're saying and feeling and even ask them to expand on why they feel that way. Once you do that, they will completely over... Up to you and look at you like you're an advisor or an authority, not a salesperson trying to sell them something. Now, if you've done your work properly, all their objections will be eliminated for the most part during the discovery process. How powerful could you be in selling if you knew very skilled questions that you asked that would immediately get your potential customer to overcome their own concerns and persuade themselves on why they wanted to do business with you rather than you trying to persuade them yourself?
Now, I'm going to give you some very powerful, very powerful trust-based questions that you can ask at the end of your conversations or even during them. Automatically put you in the front of the pack of all sales people trying to win over that account or compete for your potential customers' business. Let's just all make sure that we understand that more than likely, a few concerns may arise during the sales process. That's normal. But discuss these concerns with your potential customers. If you try to hide them or just sweep them under the rug, hoping they'll go away, it's still going to be there in their mind. Now, because of what I'm teaching you, you will have already established something that 99% of salespeople never do with their prospects. You have established.
So you will already have a good relationship with your prospect. So be open and ask if there are any concerns, and bring them to the table. If there are, talk about them as two people working together to resolve them. Now, continually check by asking things like this. Let's go to the script. Mr. Jones, are you comfortable with this? Or, Sally, how does this look to you so far? Or, Jane, do you have anything that you'd like to address to me at this point? This question is one of the most powerful questions you can ever ask because it will make you look as an authority, a trusted advisor in your prospect's mind, not a salesperson. Now, here is a three-step formula to helping them overcome their own concerns. One, we want to clarify.
Two, we want to discuss the concern. Three, we want to help them diffuse their own concern. Now, let's go over clarifying. First, we have to understand exactly what their concern is and why they have that concern. I'm going to tell you. 99% of all salespeople just assume they know and they go into objection handling mode and never ask. And then what happens? They lose the sale. And how does it make you feel when you lose that sale? Frustrated? Maybe a lowered self-esteem? Sad? Hurt? Even rejected? Never assume you know what's behind their concerns or tell them. I know how you feel because I used to feel the same way and you found it to be something different. That is old-school objection handling techniques from the stone ages of selling.
It's pretty much you saying 'yes,' but answers. Now, I guarantee you that you don't know how they feel unless they previously told you. It's easy. Just ask them. Now, here are some seven-figure clarifying questions that you can ask. So, Sally, when you say that, you know, repeat back what they said their concern was. What do you mean by that? Or here's my favorite: how do you mean by that? Or why do you ask that? Or I'm curious, Sally, why do you feel that way? This is a good one as well. Jane, can I ask you where you got that information from? Or, James, how did you arrive at thinking that way? Once you know exactly what their concern is and why they feel that way.
You're then going to discuss it with them like a friend talking to another friend, conversationally. You're going to ask them how they see themselves resolving their own concern. Now, after you've discussed the concern, you can then start diffusing their concerns and helping them overcome their concerns in their own mind. You can ask questions like these. Let's look at the script. Jane, suppose it wasn't what you thought it was. Brian, suppose you could... Kelly, what if we could? John, what if you could, and then you're going to offer an alternative view to theirs? Let's say you sell coaching services. You would say something like this. Fred, tell me, if there was a way that you could get the training that you needed, would that help you succeed?
You can see how— by never making a statement, or trying to prove them with facts to prove your point and using what you think you know to ask questions— how people can persuade themselves to look at things differently and question their beliefs on why they think that way. They will then start to persuade themselves to look at their concerns differently and even come up with ways or solutions they can overcome it. Now you can use this process with any concern that you ever encounter. I want to thank you for watching this video about how to defuse any objections that your potential customers bring up in your sales conversations. Now in the next video, you're going to learn how to ask the right committing questions, also known as closing, at the right time in the conversation that will allow your prospects to close themselves.
